title: To codify Executive Order 14096 relating to revitalizing our Nation's commitment to environmental justice for all.
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on Natural Resources, and Science, S
plain_summary: This bill would make an executive order about environmental justice into a permanent law. The law would require all government agencies to make environmental fairness part of their main job and create plans every four years to protect communities from pollution. It would also create a special White House office to focus on environmental justice and require companies to tell the public when they release dangerous chemicals. The goal is to make sure all Americans, especially those in poor and minority communities, are protected from environmental harm.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on Natural Resources, and Science, Space, and Technology, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker,
subjects: Presidents and presidential powers, Vice Presidents

sections.title: Section 1  Codification of Executive Order 14096
sections.text: Executive Order fourteen thousand ninety six (a presidential order published in the Federal Register on page twenty five thousand two hundred fifty one that focuses on renewing our country's promise to provide environmental justice for everyone) will become law with the same power as if Congress had passed it.
